ddls:
CREATE TABLE "observations" (
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the observation started */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the observation ended */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site where the observation occurred */,
  "phenomenon_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the phenomenon observed */,
  "visibility_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the visibility rating of the phenomenon */,
  "observer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observer who recorded the observation */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team associated with the observation */,
  "magnitude" REAL /* Brightness magnitude of the observed phenomenon */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation angle of the phenomenon */,
  "azimuth" REAL /* Azimuth angle of the phenomenon */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the observation */,
  "equipment_used" TEXT /* Equipment used during the observation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("observation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_observer_id FOREIGN KEY ("observer_id") REFERENCES observers ("observer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_phenomenon_id FOREIGN KEY ("phenomenon_id") REFERENCES phenomena ("phenomenon_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_visibility_id FOREIGN KEY ("visibility_id") REFERENCES visibility_ratings ("visibility_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observers" (
  "observer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the observer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the observer */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliated organization or team of the observer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the observer */,
  "expertise_level" TEXT /* Level of expertise (e.g., amateur, professional) */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the observer joined the organization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("observer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the observation site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the site */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the site */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the site */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the site */,
  "light_pollution_index" REAL /* Light pollution index at the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the team operates */,
  "leader_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team leader */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the team (e.g., email, phone) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teams_leader_id FOREIGN KEY ("leader_id") REFERENCES observers ("observer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "phenomena" (
  "phenomenon_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each phenomenon */,
  "description" TEXT /* A detailed description of the phenomenon */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the phenomenon (e.g., meteor, fireball, comet) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the phenomenon (e.g., common, rare) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("phenomenon_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visibility_ratings" (
  "visibility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visibility rating */,
  "visibility_name" TEXT /* Name of the visibility rating (e.g., I, II, III, IV) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the visibility rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visibility_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observation_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated observation */,
  "note" TEXT /* Text of the note */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observation_notes_observation_id FOREIGN KEY ("observation_id") REFERENCES observations ("observation_id")
)
column_names: Date and time when the observation started
Date and time when the observation ended
Reference to the site where the observation occurred
Reference to the visibility rating of the phenomenon
ID of the observer who recorded the observation
ID of the team associated with the observation
Brightness magnitude of the observed phenomenon

Date when the observer joined the organization

Contact information for the team (e.g., email, phone)
A detailed description of the phenomenon
Category of the phenomenon (e.g., meteor, fireball, comet)
Frequency of the phenomenon (e.g., common, rare)
Unique identifier for each visibility rating
Name of the visibility rating (e.g., I, II, III, IV)
Detailed description of the visibility rating
Date and time when the note was created
Date and time when the note was last updated
